sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Athletic Club has struggled in their last 5 matches, managing only 2 wins and suffering heavy defeats against top teams like Real Madrid (0-3) and Barcelona (4-0). In contrast, Atlético Madrid has shown better consistency with 4 wins in their last 5, including a narrow 3-1 loss to Barcelona, indicating their competitive edge.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters between these sides, Atlético Madrid has dominated with two 1-0 victories, showcasing their ability to edge out Athletic Club in tight games. Athletic Club has failed to score in both matches, highlighting their struggle against Atlético's defensive setup.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Athletic Club, sitting 8th with 20 points, has scored 14 goals but conceded 20, showing defensive frailties. Atlético Madrid, in 4th with 31 points, boasts 28 goals scored and only 14 conceded, underlining their attacking prowess and solid backline.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: For Athletic Club, Nico Williams (7.14 average rating) poses a threat with his dribbling (2.5 per game, 3rd in league), while Atlético's Julián Álvarez (7 goals, 7.09 rating) is in fine form, likely to exploit Athletic's defensive gaps.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Athletic Club's high penalty area entries (31.3 per game, 2nd in league) suggest they’ll press for chances, but their low conversion rate (32.3%) could hurt them. Atlético’s knack for scoring early (8 goals in first 15 minutes) and late (8 goals in 76-90 minutes) may catch Athletic off guard.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Atlético’s superior form, head-to-head advantage, and Sofascore’s prediction (50% away win probability), they are favorites to win. Athletic Club’s home advantage at San Mamés might keep it close, but their recent struggles tilt the balance towards Atlético.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Athletic Club and Atlético Madrid at San Mamés ended in a goalless draw, with both teams showcasing moments of promise but failing to convert their chances. Neither side managed to break the deadlock despite creating opportunities, reflecting a tightly contested battle. Athletic Club showed more intent in terms of big chances created, though they squandered key opportunities. Atlético Madrid, on the other hand, held a slight edge in possession and appeared more composed in duels, winning a higher percentage of physical battles. Discipline has been an issue for the home side, as they picked up more bookings compared to their opponents.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in attack shifted several times during the first half, indicating a game of fluctuating dominance. Atlético Madrid started with early control, dictating the tempo in the opening stages with consistent attacking spells. However, Athletic Club responded strongly with a prolonged period of pressure, particularly in the middle of the half, where they mounted several dangerous forays forward. Towards the latter stages, the away side regained some initiative with renewed attacking intent, while the home team also had their moments to push back. This back-and-forth dynamic suggests both teams are capable of turning the tide in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performers and missed opportunities
halftimeSections.text: For Athletic Club, players like Gorka Guruzeta and Oihan Sancet had notable involvement in the final third but failed to capitalize on big chances, which could have given them the lead. Their ability to penetrate the penalty area was evident, yet finishing let them down. On the other side, Thiago Almada stood out for Atlético Madrid with a significant presence in attack, though he too missed a crucial opportunity. Julián Alvarez also played a key role in creating chances for the away team, showing his potential to unlock defenses. Both teams will need their key players to be more clinical in the second half if they are to find the net.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Athletic Club demonstrated a willingness to push forward with frequent entries into the final third and penalty area, suggesting an aggressive approach at home. However, their inability to convert chances and vulnerability in duels could be exploited by a more physical Atlético Madrid side. The away team, with better control in aerial and ground duels, appears to rely on structured play and could look to capitalize on counter-attacks. The disciplinary issues for the home team might also play a factor, as they risk further cautions or even a sending-off if the intensity rises. Both managers may consider tactical adjustments to gain an edge in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see an increase in urgency from both sides as they chase a breakthrough. Athletic Club, buoyed by the home crowd, will probably continue their attacking intent and look to make the most of their chances in the final third. Atlético Madrid, with their slight edge in possession and physicality, might aim to exploit spaces left by the home side’s forward pushes. Given the chances created in the first half, it feels like a goal is on the horizon, with either team capable of striking first. The outcome could hinge on which side sharpens their finishing and maintains composure under pressure.
